title: ufreeh
text: ufreeh Free (delete) a handle. long ufreeh (UHACT* hAny); Copy Parameters hAny A handle to an environment, instance, operation, parameter, entity, occurrence, or item list. This value is set to zero if the operation was successful. Note that this parameter is passed by reference, even though it is an input parameter Return Values For more information, see the "Call-In API Return Codes" topic. Use Library: ucall Description ufreeh is used to free (delete) a handle and the resources allocated to it. A handle can be an environment handle (as returned by uecreate or ueopen ), an instance handle (as returned by uinstnew or uinstopn ), an operation handle (as returned by uinstopr ), an entity handle (as returned by uprmgeth ), an occurrence handle (as returned by uentcreo ) or an item list (as returned by ulistnew ). You must always free a handle, even if the object owning that handle has already been deleted. For example, you might be calling uedelete(hEnv) to shut down an environment, but it is still necessary to call ufreeh(&hEnv) afterwards. Likewise, uinstdel(hIn, &procErr) deletes the instance, but not the instance handle, so you should call ufreeh(&hIn) afterwards. Example: uecreate, uedelete, ufreeh, uinstdel, uinstnew, uinstopr, and uopract The following simple call-in program shows how to activate the EXEC operation of the service MYSVC. As usual, error checking has been omitted for the sake of brevity. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> void main (void) { UHACT hEnv; /* Uniface environment */ UHACT hIn; /* Instance handle */ UHACT hOp; /* Operation handle */ long opStat; /* Activate status return parameter */ /* Create the environment */ uecreate(1, 0, 0, "my.asn", 0, 0, &hEnv); /* Instantiate service MYSVC */ uinstnew(hEnv, "MYSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, 0); uinstopr(hIn, "EXEC", 0, &hOp); /* Instantiate the EXEC operation */ uopract (hOp, &opStat, 0); /* Activate the operation */ uinstdel(hIn, 0); /* Destroy the instance */ ufreeh (&hOp); /* Free the operation */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free the instance */ uedelete(hEnv, -1); /* Shut down the environment */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free the environment handle */ return 0; // on success } Copy Related Links Call-In API Return Codes Codes that can possibly be returned by functions from the 3GL call-in API are consistent with the values returned by the ProcScript function $procerror, unless noted otherwise. uecreate Initialize the Uniface runtime for component call-in. uentcreo Create an occurrence of an entity parameter, and make it current. ueopen Return the current Uniface environment handle that can be used to instantiate services. uinstnew Create a new instance of a Uniface 4GL component. uinstopn Get a handle to an existing component instance. uinstopr Return the handle to an operation. ulistnew Create an item list. uprmgeth Get the handle of the specified operation parameter or entity field. Or detach a parameter from an operation. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
